The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: CHAPTER 4 : GLOBALISATION AND THE INDIAN ECONOMY, GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISATION TION TION TION TION, CHAPTER 4, PRODUCTION ACROSS COUNTRIES. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Globalisation and the Indian Economy is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that nOTES FOR THE TEACHER NOTES FOR THE TEACHER NOTES FOR THE TEACHER NOTES FOR THE TEACHER NOTES FOR THE TEACHER Most regions of the world are getting increasingly interconnected.

The text explains that while this interconnectedness across countries has many dimensions — cultural, political, social and economic — this chapter looks at globalisation in a more limited sense.

The text explains that it defines globalisation as the integration between countries through foreign trade and foreign investments by multinational corporations (MNCs).

The text explains that as you will notice, the more complex issues of portfolio investment have been left out.

The text explains that if we look at the past thirty years or so, we find that MNCs have been a major force in the globalisation process connecting distant regions of the world.

The text explains that why are the MNCs spreading their production to other countries and what are the ways in which they are doing so?

The text explains that the first part of the chapter discusses this.

The text explains that rather than relying on quantitative estimates, the rapid rise and influence of the MNCs has been shown through a variety of examples, mainly drawn from the Indian context.

The text explains that note that the examples are an aid to explain a more general point.

The text explains that while teaching, the emphasis should be on the ideas and examples are to be used as illustrations.

The text explains that you can also creatively use comprehension passages like the one given after Section II to test and reinforce new concepts.

The text explains that integration of production and integration of markets is a key idea behind understanding the process of globalisation and its impact.

The text explains that this has been dealt with at length in this chapter, highlighting the role of MNCs in the process.

The text explains that you have to ensure that the students grasp this idea with sufficient clarity, before moving on to the next topic.

The text explains that globalisation has been facilitated by several factors.

The text explains that three of these have been highlighted: rapid improvements in technology, liberalisation of trade and investment policies and, pressures from international organisations such as the WTO.

The text explains that improvement in technology is a fascinating area for students and you may, with a few directions, encourage them to do their own explorations.

The text explains that to what extent has globalisation contributed to the development process?

The text explains that this section draws on the topics covered in Chapters 1 and 2 (for example, what is a fair development goal), which you can refer to.

The text explains that this might include contexts that have not been covered in the chapter, such as the impact of imports on local farmers, etc.

The text explains that sources for Information The call for a fairer globalisation has been given, among others, by the International Labour Organisation — www.ilo.org.

The text explains that another interesting resource is the WTO website http://www.wto.org.

The text explains that g G G G GLLLLLOBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISATION TION TION TION TION AND AND AND AND AND THE THE THE THE THE IIIIINDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN E E E E ECONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY 55 55 55 55 55 GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISATION TION TION TION TION AND THE INDIAN ECONOMY AND THE INDIAN ECONOMY AND THE INDIAN ECONOMY AND THE INDIAN ECONOMY AND THE INDIAN ECONOMY CHAPTER 4 As consumers in today’s world, some of us have a wide choice of goods and services before us.

The text explains that a similar explosion of brands can be seen for many other goods: from shirts to televisions to processed fruit juices.

The text explains that we shall dwell on these questions in this chapter.

The text explains that mNCs set up offices and factories for production in regions where they can get cheap labour and other resources.

The text explains that g G G G GLLLLLOBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISATION TION TION TION TION AND AND AND AND AND THE THE THE THE THE IIIIINDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN E E E E ECONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY 57 57 57 57 57 Complete the following statement to show how the production process in the garment industry is spread across countries.

The text explains that we dissect the manufacturing process and look for the best solution at each step.

The text explains that the production process is divided into small parts and spread out across the globe.

The text explains that in addition, MNCs might look for government policies that look after their interests.

The text explains that you will read more about the policies later in the chapter.

The text explains that in fact, many of the top MNCs have wealth exceeding the entire budgets of the developing country governments.

The text explains that g G G G GLLLLLOBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISATION TION TION TION TION AND AND AND AND AND THE THE THE THE THE IIIIINDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN E E E E ECONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY 59 59 59 59 59 Read the passage on the left and answer the questions.

The text explains that discuss the following factors: (a) cost of labour and other resources in India (b) the presence of several local manufacturers who supply auto- parts to Ford Motors (c) closeness to a large number of buyers in India and China 5.

The text explains that in history you would have read about the trade routes connecting India and South Asia to markets both in the East and West and the extensive trade that took place along these routes.

The text explains that g G G G GLLLLLOBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISATION TION TION TION TION AND AND AND AND AND THE THE THE THE THE IIIIINDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN E E E E ECONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY 61 61 61 61 61 1.

The text explains that explain how the import of steel by China will affect.

The text explains that 62 62 62 62 62 U U U U UNDERST NDERST NDERST NDERST NDERSTANDING ANDING ANDING ANDING ANDING E E E E ECONOMIC CONOMIC CONOMIC CONOMIC CONOMIC D D D D DEVEL EVEL EVEL EVEL EVELOPMENT OPMENT OPMENT OPMENT OPMENT Technology Rapid improvement in technology has been one major factor that has stimulated the globalisation process.

The text explains that globalisation is this process of rapid integration or interconnection between countries.

The text explains that mNCs are playing a major role in the globalisation process.

The text explains that what is the role of MNCs in the globalisation process?

The text explains that g G G G GLLLLLOBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISA OBALISATION TION TION TION TION AND AND AND AND AND THE THE THE THE THE IIIIINDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN NDIAN E E E E ECONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY CONOMY 63 63 63 63 63 amazing world of internet, where you can obtain and share information on almost anything you want to know.

The text explains that suppose the Indian government puts a tax on import of toys.

The text explains that governments can use trade barriers to increase or decrease (regulate) foreign trade and to decide what kinds of goods and how much of each, should come into the country.

The text explains that (c) goods, investments and people between countries.

The text explains that (ii) The most common route for investments by MNCs in countries around the world is to (a) set up new factories.

The text explains that (iii) Globalisation has led to improvement in living conditions (a) of all the people (b) of people in the developed countries (c) of workers in the developing countries (d) none of the above I.

The text explains that take some branded products that we use everyday (soaps, toothpaste, garments, electronic goods, etc.).

The text explains that take any Indian industry or service of your choice.

The text explains that collect information and photographs from newspapers, magazine clippings, books, television, internet, interviews with people on the following aspects of the industry.

The text explains that (i) Various producers/companies in the industry (ii) Is the product exported to other countries?

prelims_traps: Do not treat CHAPTER 4 : GLOBALISATION AND THE INDIAN ECONOMY and GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISA GLOBALISATION TION TION TION TION as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Globalisation and the Indian Economy; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
